c1.summary: Malignant luminal epithelial cells that proliferate uncontrollably, marked by the receptor tyrosine kinase ERBB2.

c3.summary: Innate immune phagocytes infiltrating breast tumors, expressing LYZ (antimicrobial enzyme), FCER1G (Fc receptor signaling).

c4.summary: Contractile basal cells lining breast ducts that suppress stromal invasion and maintain epithelial polarity.

c5.summary: Adaptive immune lymphocytes that infiltrate breast tumors and mediate antitumor cytotoxicity.

c6.summary: Antibody-producing lymphocytes that infiltrate breast tumors and signal better patient survival, marked by MS4A1.

c9.summary: Tissue-resident immune cells that store and release proteases.

c2.summary: Stromal fibroblasts in human breast tissue are mesenchymal cells that build and remodel the extracellular matrix.

c7.summary: Endothelial cells line the blood and lymphatic vessels that supply the breast tumor microenvironment, regulating perfusion, nutrient delivery, and immune cell trafficking. AQP1 marks water-channel activity in vascular endothelium.

c8.summary: Plasmacytoid dendritic cells are rare innate immune sentinels in the breast tumor microenvironment that specialize in producing type I interferons to shape antitumor immunity. TCL1A and LILRA4 are canonical surface markers of this lineage.
